system_prompt_verbatim: You are an isolated benchmark agent. The only valid scope of your work is the current working directory. Do not read or write any path outside the cwd. Do not consult external memory, skills, MCPs, or project context from parent directories. Treat the task description below as the sole specification. Do not ask questions; produce the requested artifact(s) directly. When the task is complete, stop.

args.command:
# Extract context around each line to check for draft/preliminary/superseded markers
for line_num in 833 1665 2497 3329 4851; do
  echo "=== Line $line_num ==="
  sed -n "$((line_num-2)),$((line_num+2))p" ./haystack.txt
  echo ""
done
args.description: Read context around each confirmed treasury vault code entry

args.command:
# Search for draft/preliminary/superseded entries for each entity
for entity in "Westcairn" "Xenmore" "Ystwyth" "Zoarwick" "Anvilford"; do
  echo "=== Checking for draft/preliminary/superseded entries for $entity ==="
  grep -n -i "draft\|preliminary\|superseded" ./haystack.txt | grep -i "$entity" || echo "No draft/preliminary/superseded entries found"
done

model_output: Perfect! I've found all 5 confirmed treasury vault code entries. Let me now extract the values, sort them, and compute the SHA-1 hash.

args.command:
# Create the sorted pipe-joined string and compute SHA-1
python3 << 'EOF'
import hashlib

# Confirmed entries (entity: value)
entries = {
    "Westcairn": "TV-8014",
    "Xenmore": "TV-2207",
    "Ystwyth": "TV-9933",
    "Zoarwick": "TV-5582",
    "Anvilford": "TV-7740"
}

# Sort alphabetically by entity name
sorted_entries = sorted(entries.items())

# Format as entity=value pairs
pairs = [f"{entity}={value}" for entity, value in sorted_entries]

# Join with pipe separator
joined_string = "|".join(pairs)

print(f"Joined string: {joined_string}")

# Compute SHA-1 hash
sha1_hash = hashlib.sha1(joined_string.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
print(f"SHA-1 hash: {sha1_hash}")
EOF
args.description: Compute SHA-1 hash of sorted treasury vault codes
